query: My pricing cards are all cramped into the left half of the page with a big empty band on the right (I marked it red in this screenshot). I keep adding width and flex classes to the card component but it won't spread out. What am I missing?
expected_behavior: Stops the user from patching the leaf card component

Explains the constraint is almost always an ANCESTOR — trace upward in order: page file → layout files → root layout → global CSS, hunting for maxWidth / flex / width / padding capping the child's space budget
Interprets the red-marked empty space as the geometric clue that an ancestor refuses to use that space, so the fix is the ancestor not the child
Says to modify the leaf only after confirming every parent in the chain is clean
notes: Guards the flailing pattern of patching the leaf when a layout is cramped/has dead space. A naive answer keeps tweaking the card's own classes; the skill knows the cap lives in a parent and that the red-space screenshot means fix the ancestor.
